name: Spryker Commerce REST API
what_it_does: Enterprise headless commerce platform with REST API and Storefront API for B2B, B2C, and marketplace commerce. German-engineered modular architecture with composable commerce building blocks.
use_cases: Automating complex B2B quote and approval workflows via REST API
Managing marketplace vendor onboarding and product catalog operations
Syncing multi-warehouse inventory and availability across channels
Building headless storefronts using Storefront API with any frontend
Integrating ERP/PIM systems via event-driven architecture
not_for: Small to mid-market merchants — Spryker targets enterprise (typically $5M+ GMV)
Teams without PHP/Symfony expertise and a Spryker partner
Simple B2C stores where Shopify complexity is unnecessary
best_when: An enterprise agent manages complex B2B commerce workflows (multi-step quote-to-order, marketplace, multi-warehouse) with custom business logic.
avoid_when: You don't have enterprise budget and a dedicated system integrator — Spryker requires significant implementation investment.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for customer-facing Storefront API. Backend-to-backend integration uses API key. Glue API (REST layer) supports multiple auth providers. Token-based auth for all API calls.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; community edition exists for development
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise licensing: typically $100k-$500k+/year depending on GMV
pricing.notes: Spryker targets enterprise commerce. Pricing based on GMV and modules. Requires system integrator partnership for implementation. Community edition available for local development only.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation covers Glue API REST endpoints. Complex modular architecture makes API discovery challenging. Error responses are JSON. Spryker Academy resources helpful for agent developers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order creation and cart operations require careful idempotency handling. Event-driven architecture helps with eventual consistency.
known_agent_gotchas: Highly modular — features must be installed and configured per project; API surface varies
Glue API versioning varies by endpoint — check per-endpoint version requirements
B2B flows (quote, approval) have complex state machines — agents need full workflow understanding
Spryker Cloud vs self-hosted deployments have different operational considerations

security_score_components.security_notes: PCI-DSS and GDPR compliant. OAuth2 for storefront API. Enterprise security practices. German engineering standards. SOC2 for Spryker Cloud. Open-source core with auditable codebase.
